content: In the context of the effect of drainage density on the run-off generation and the hydrograph at the catchment outlet, all other factors remaining the same, pick one or more CORRECT statement(s) from the choices given below.

options.content: Lower drainage density results in higher peak in flood hydrograph compared to that when the drainage density is higher.
Lower drainage density results in lower peak in flood hydrograph compared to that when the drainage density is higher.
Lower drainage density results in a flood hydrograph with a longer time base compared to that when the drainage density is higher.
Lower drainage density results in a flood hydrograph with a shorter time base compared to that when the drainage density is higher.

content:
To derive the total flood hydrograph at a catchment outlet from an isolated storm, the order in which the following methods are applied, from the first method to the last method, is

P. Obtaining the hyetograph

Q. Addition of baseflow

R. Estimation of initial and infiltration losses

S. Application of unit hydrograph
explanation:
To obtain the flood hydrograph, the methods would be arranged as PRSQ.

P. Obtaining the hyetograph

R. Estimation of initial and infiltration losses

S. Application of unit hydrograph

Q. Addition of base flow

content:
The ordinates of a one-hour unit hydrograph for a catchment are given below: 

$$ \begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|} \hline t(\text { hour }) & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 & 7 \\ \hline Q\left(\mathrm{~m}^3 / \mathrm{s}\right) & 0 & 9 & 21 & 18 & 12 & 5 & 2 & 0 \\ \hline \end{array} $$


Using the principle of superposition, a D-hour unit hydrograph for the catchment was derived from this one-hour unit hydrograph. The ordinates of the D-hour unit hydrograph were obtained as 3 m3/s at t = 1 hour and 10 m3/s at t = 2 hour. The value of D (in integer) is _____________.
explanation:
$$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|} \hline \text { Time (t) } & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 & 7 & & \\ \hline \begin{array}{c} \text { Discharge (Q) } \\ \mathbf{1}^{\text {st }} \mathbf{h r} \text { UH } \end{array} & 0 & 9 & 21 & 18 & 12 & 5 & 2 & 0 & & \\ \hline \mathbf{2}^{\text {nd }} \mathbf{h r} \text { UH } & - & 0 & 9 & 21 & 18 & 12 & 5 & 2 & 0 & \\ \hline \mathbf{3}^{\text {rd }} \mathbf{h r} \text { UH } & - & - & 0 & 9 & 21 & 18 & 12 & 5 & 2 & 0 \\ \hline \end{array} $$

From table clearly visible $\mathrm{D}=3 \mathrm{hr}$ or $3-\mathrm{hr} \mathrm{UH}$ is $3 \mathrm{~m}^3 / \mathrm{sec}$

Hence, the correct answer is 3 .

content:
The peak of flood hydrograph due to a 3 -hour duration storm in a catchment is $180 \mathrm{~m}^3 / \mathrm{s}$. The total rainfall depth is 6.6 cm . It can be assumed that the average infiltration loss is $0.2 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{h}$. There are no other losses. The base flow is constant at a value of $30 \mathrm{~m}^3 / \mathrm{s}$.

The peak value of the 3-hour unit hydrograph for this catchment (in $\mathrm{m}^3 / \mathrm{s}$ ) is ________ (round off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
To calculate the peak value of the 3-hour unit hydrograph for the catchment, first consider the given values:

Peak Discharge: $ 180 \, \mathrm{m}^3/\mathrm{s} $

Base Flow: $ 30 \, \mathrm{m}^3/\mathrm{s} $

Phi-index (infiltration rate): $ 0.2 \, \mathrm{cm}/\mathrm{h} $

Total Rainfall Depth, $ R $: $ 6.6 \, \mathrm{cm} $

The equation for calculating the peak of the 3-hour unit hydrograph is:

$ Q_P = \frac{\text{Peak Discharge} - \text{Base Flow}}{R - \phi t} $

Where:

$ \phi $ is the phi-index (average infiltration loss),

$ t $ is the storm duration in hours.

Substitute the given values into the equation:

$ Q_P = \frac{180 - 30}{6.6 - 0.2 \times 3} $

First, simplify the denominator:

$ 6.6 - 0.2 \times 3 = 6.6 - 0.6 = 6.0 \, \mathrm{cm} $

Now calculate:

$ Q_P = \frac{150}{6} = 25 \, \mathrm{m}^3/\mathrm{s} $

Therefore, the peak value of the 3-hour unit hydrograph for this catchment is 25 $\mathrm{m}^3/\mathrm{s}$.
